mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS path keeps discount rates low and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is not tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above the unconditional baseline when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the sign flips negative in more than 55% of non-tightening observations.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above the Fed's likely comfort zone forces faster policy tightening expectations, raising real discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which predicts negative 20-day SPX returns.
regime_hint: inflation scare within a hiking cycle
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after inflation acceleration is not below the sample median.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and softer household income growth, which reduces earnings expectations and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the conditional mean return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which supports earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 50% over triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable labor conditions indicates broad-based production momentum and improving cyclical profits, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE is unchanged or lower versus 3 months ago
failure_condition: Reject if joint industrial-strength and stable-labor episodes do not outperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if the signal becomes negative when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in HOUST reflects stronger housing demand and positive wealth effects, which improves cyclical sentiment and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the conditional hit rate is not materially above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL falls while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy rates tighten mechanically and equity multiples compress, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation, restrictive-policy episodes produce non-negative 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the mean forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor market, a falling PAYEMS count together with rising UNRATE signals job-market deterioration that reduces risk appetite and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll declines and unemployment rises are not followed by below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in most triggered cases.
